zones.label: Layer 5 — Application (Interface)
zones.summary: Where humans see data, set rules, and take action

zones.label: Layer 4 — Cloud Platform
zones.summary: Receives, stores, routes, and processes streams of sensor data
facts: Components: MQTT brokers, Cloud IoT services, Time-series databases
Adafruit IO · HiveMQ · AWS IoT Core

zones.label: Layer 3 — Edge Processing
zones.summary: Processes data close to the source — low latency, works offline
facts: Components: Local decision-making, Data filtering, Rapid response
Pico W running a MicroPython web server

zones.label: Layer 2 — Network (Connectivity)
zones.summary: Moves data reliably between devices and the internet

zones.label: Layer 1 — Perception (Things)
zones.summary: Interacts with the physical world — collects data and drives outputs

quiz.question: Which layer holds the DHT22 temperature sensor, the HC-SR04 distance sensor, and the Raspberry Pi Pico W?
quiz.explanation: Layer 1 — Perception (Things) is where sensors, actuators, and microcontrollers interact with the physical world to collect data and drive outputs.

quiz.question: Where do humans see the data, set rules, and take action — for example through a Grafana or Adafruit IO dashboard, or a mobile app?
quiz.explanation: Layer 5 — Application (Interface) is the top layer: dashboards, mobile apps, and alerts (Adafruit IO, Node-RED, Grafana, Home Assistant, Email / SMS).

quiz.question: Which layer moves data reliably between devices and the internet using WiFi 802.11 b/g/n, Bluetooth BLE 5.x, LoRa 915 MHz, and Ethernet?
quiz.explanation: Layer 2 — Network (Connectivity) provides the wireless radios, gateways, and routers that carry data between devices and the internet.

quiz.question: Which layer does a Pico W running a MicroPython web server with local threshold alerts (no cloud needed) belong to?
quiz.explanation: Layer 3 — Edge Processing handles local decision-making, data filtering, and rapid response close to the source, giving low latency and offline operation.

quiz.question: Which layer uses MQTT brokers and time-series databases such as AWS IoT Core, Mosquitto, and InfluxDB to receive, store, and route streams of sensor data?
quiz.explanation: Layer 4 — Cloud Platform receives, stores, routes, and processes streams of sensor data using MQTT brokers, cloud IoT services, and time-series databases.
